title: USS Milius conducts Taiwan Strait transit demonstrating freedom of navigation
summary: US 7th Fleet announces USS Milius (DDG-69) conducted a routine Taiwan Strait transit on May 1, demonstrating the US commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
contents:
# USS Milius Conducts Taiwan Strait Transit

## Transit Details
The US 7th Fleet announced that USS Milius (DDG-69) conducted a routine Taiwan Strait transit on May 1, 2026, through waters where high seas freedoms of navigation and overflight apply in accordance with international law.

## Vessel Information
- USS Milius (DDG-69)
- Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer
- Forward-deployed to Yokosuka, Japan

## Strategic Messaging
The 7th Fleet stated: "Milius' transit demonstrates the United States' commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific. The United States military flies, sails, and operates anywhere international law allows."

## Operational Context
The transit occurs during elevated PLA Navy activity around Taiwan, including Liaoning carrier strike group operations in the Philippine Sea. US Navy Taiwan Strait transits serve as strategic signaling of continued American commitment to freedom of navigation.

## Expected Response
China's PLA Eastern Theater Command and Ministry of National Defense typically respond to Taiwan Strait transits with diplomatic protests and monitoring announcements.

## Tags
us-navy, taiwan-strait, freedom-of-navigation, 7th-fleet, ddg-69, indo-pacific
